title: regression: SELinux context
body:
podman-0.9.3.1-1.git1cd906d.fc29 has some SELinux regressions:
```
# podman run --rm alpine cat /proc/self/attr/current                                                             
unconfined_u:system_r:spc_t:s0     <-- expected container_t

# podman run --rm --security-opt label=type:svirt_qemu_net_t alpine cat /proc/self/attr/current
unconfined_u:system_r:spc_t:s0     <-- expected svirt_qemu_net_t

# podman run --rm --security-opt label=level:s0:c1,c2 alpine cat /proc/self/attr/current
unconfined_u:system_r:spc_t:s0     <-- expected container_t:s0:c1,c2
```
container-selinux-2.73-2.gitd7a3f33.fc29

comments.body: This would indicate to me that podman was built without SELinux support?
Or we have selinux disabled in libpod.conf
Ed do you have label=true in your libpod.conf file?

I'm not sure how to answer that because until now I was unaware of any libpod.conf. I see none under `/etc`:
```
# find /etc/ -name libpod.conf
[no results]
```
The podman RPM ships several files matching 'conf', but the string 'label' does not appear in any of them, nor have any of those files been touched by me:
```
# # rpm -ql podman|grep conf | while read f; do echo $f; grep label $f;done
/etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-bridge.conflist
/usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/podman.conf
/usr/share/containers/libpod.conf
/usr/share/man/man5/containers-mounts.conf.5.gz
/usr/share/man/man5/libpod.conf.5.gz

# rpm -V podman
[no output]
```
@rhatdan does this answer your question? Is there anything else I can check?

Works For Me.
```
 dnf update --enablerepo=updates-testing podman
```

```
# grep label /usr/share/containers/libpod.conf 
# Default libpod support for container labeling
# label=true
```

You're on f28; can you try f29? 

Supplementary info: the version I was testing (0.9.3.**1**-1.git1cd906d) was not the same as Dan's (0.9.**3**-3.gitc3a0874). I've upgraded my f28 and f29 systems, rerun automated and manual tests. f28 does not exhibit the behavior reported in this issue; f29 does. (f28 exhibits a different problem, with --privileged, but I'm not going to dive into that today).
Cannot reproduce on fresh f29 virt; closing. Opened #1575 as new issue on both f28 and 29.
